nodes.description: The Marlin kernel transforms quantization throughput dramatically: Marlin-AWQ achieves 741 tok/s vs standard AWQ's 68 tok/s (10.9x speedup), and Marlin-GPTQ achieves 712 tok/s vs GPTQ's 276 tok/s (2.6x). This shows inference kernel design is equally critical as quantization algorithm selection.

nodes.label: Structured pruning preferred in production; FASP can prune LLaMA-30B in 20 min on single GPU
nodes.description: 82% of enterprises (Forrester 2024) prefer structured pruning for production deployment due to hardware compatibility. FASP (ICLR 2025) enables joint layer pruning of LLaMA-30B in 20 minutes on a single RTX 4090, 15x faster than prior methods. NVIDIA TensorRT 9.2 and Apple Core ML 7.0 support pruning + quantization together.

nodes.label: SYNTHESIS: Five Pillars of LLM Inference Cost Reduction
nodes.description: The research identifies five mutually reinforcing pillars for reducing LLM inference cost in production: (1) Model Compression & Quantization, (2) Efficient Serving & Batching, (3) Prompt & Context Optimization, (4) Model Routing & Cascading, and (5) Hardware & Cloud Cost Strategy. Maximum savings require combining techniques across pillars rather than treating them as alternatives.

nodes.label: SYNTHESIS: Quantization Is the Highest-ROI Single Intervention
nodes.description: INT4 quantization (AWQ/GPTQ + Marlin kernel) delivers the strongest single-step cost reduction: 75% GPU memory reduction, 2.7–2.9x GPU speedup, up to 8x hardware cost reduction, with accuracy loss below practical detection thresholds (<4% perplexity, <8% code accuracy). Kernel choice (Marlin) is as important as quantization algorithm — wrong kernel selection can forfeit up to 10.9x of potential speedup. Zero model retraining required.

nodes.label: SYNTHESIS: Serving Infrastructure Is a Hidden Cost Multiplier
nodes.description: Serving framework choices have multiplicative effects on cost independent of model compression. Continuous batching (up to 23x throughput vs. static), PagedAttention (KV memory waste from 60–80% to <4%), and advanced kernels (FlashAttention-3 at 85% H100 utilization) together can reduce effective per-token cost by an order of magnitude. SGLang now leads vLLM on throughput benchmarks (3.1x on Llama-70B); TGI entered maintenance mode in 2025 and should not be chosen for new deployments.

nodes.label: SYNTHESIS: Routing & Cascading Are Under-Exploited Cost Levers
nodes.description: LLM routing (RouteLLM: 40–85% cost reduction at 95% GPT-4 quality) and model cascading (FrugalGPT / AutoMix) are among the highest-leverage interventions for organizations using frontier API models, requiring no model changes. MoE architectures (Mixtral: 1/5 compute of Llama-2-70B at equivalent quality) internalize routing at the architecture level. Unified cascade routing is theoretically optimal and outperforms routing or cascading alone; quality estimators are the critical bottleneck.

nodes.label: SYNTHESIS: Key Tension — Quantization Speedup vs. Task-Specific Accuracy
nodes.description: A fundamental tension exists between quantization's impressive throughput/memory gains and its task-specific accuracy risks. Perplexity-based evaluations (showing <4% degradation) may understate accuracy loss on downstream tasks like code generation (7.6% accuracy drop), mathematical reasoning, or low-resource language generation. The gap between benchmark-level confidence (0.92) and real-world applicability depends heavily on task type — this is a primary research gap.

nodes.label: SYNTHESIS: Distillation and Pruning Are High-Reward but High-Cost Interventions
nodes.description: Knowledge distillation (2–8x speedup, 21–31% task improvement over direct prompting of the same small model) and structured pruning (FASP: 30B model in 20 min on one GPU) offer transformative gains but require training infrastructure and domain expertise. These are best suited to organizations with high-volume, stable task distributions. Unlike quantization (post-training, zero-shot), distillation/pruning create new model artifacts requiring full MLOps pipelines. Pruning data (Forrester 2024 enterprise preferences) carries lower confidence (0.78) than quantization benchmarks.

nodes.label: SYNTHESIS: Research Gaps Identified Post-Review
nodes.description: Five gaps remain underexplored: (1) Compound stacking — quantitative evidence for combining all five pillars simultaneously is sparse; (2) Task-specific quantization accuracy degradation taxonomy beyond perplexity; (3) Router training data requirements and cold-start behavior in real deployments; (4) Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) models for distillation/pruning including engineering labor; (5) Prompt compression and prefix caching impact data are absent from this review's evidence base despite being named research areas.

nodes.label: Re-investigate: Frontier AI Safety Frameworks (Industry)
nodes.description: User flagged 'Frontier AI Safety Frameworks (Industry)' for re-investigation.

nodes.label: How does agent environment affect token efficiency?
nodes.description: The central research question: how does the environment an agent operates in (context window size, tool availability, task structure, feedback loops, multi-agent setup, etc.) affect its ability to use tokens efficiently?

nodes.label: Context window constraints and token budget management
nodes.description: How do context window limits, context compression, and token budget allocation strategies affect efficient token use in agents?

nodes.label: Tool availability and action space design for token efficiency
nodes.description: How does the set of tools available to an agent (APIs, retrievers, code executors) and how action spaces are designed affect how many tokens are needed to complete tasks?

nodes.label: Task structure, feedback loops, and prompt design efficiency
nodes.description: How do task decomposition, prompt engineering, chain-of-thought depth, and feedback loop design impact the number of tokens agents consume per unit of useful output?

nodes.label: Multi-agent and memory architecture effects on token efficiency
nodes.description: How do multi-agent setups, external memory, retrieval-augmented generation, and agent orchestration patterns influence cumulative token usage across an agent system?

nodes.label: Observation and state representation efficiency in agent environments
nodes.description: How does the way an agent's environment exposes state and observations (raw vs. structured, verbose vs. compact, grounded vs. abstract) affect token consumption and reasoning quality?
